text: This is not a melomacarouna. This is a dry biscuit.
author: Elisabet

text: It's been years, and just one day he appears at your doorstep, you let him in, and he tells a story about where he's been all those years he's been gone, then the wind will blow him away again....
BUT YOU WERE NOT HERE, I ALONE OCCUPY THE DARKNESS
You must take your opponent into a deep dark forest where 2+2=5, and the path leading out is only wide enough for one.
author: Mikhail Tal

text: Love says I'm everything. Wisdom says I'm nothing. Between the 2 my life flows.
The perfect man employs his mind as a mirror — grasping nothing, refusing nothing, receiving but not keeping.
author: Jiddu Krishnamurti

text: The more I learn, the less I know.
author: Socrates

text: Truth is not what you want it to be; it is what it is, and you must bend to its power or live a lie.
author: Miyamoto Musashi

text: The mind and what is are not two separate processes.
author: J. Krishnamurti

text: The meaning of life is just to be alive. It is so plain and so obvious and so simple. And yet, everybody rushes around in a great panic as if it were necessary to achieve something beyond themselves.
author: Alan Watts

text: At the edge of expectation. Assumptions become belief.
author: Andrei Jikh

text: You Will Know Them by Their Fruits.
author: Matthew

text: Beware that, when fighting monsters, you yourself do not become a monster. For when you gaze long into the abyss, the abyss gazes also into you.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: The thing we tell of can never be found by seeking, yet only seekers find it.
author: Bayazid Bastami

text: Om is the bow.
Wake up, grow up, clean up, show up.
author: Ken Wilber

text: We must master the art of peace in addition to the art of war.
author: Master Roshi

text: However necessary it may be to say 'I' and 'mine' for the practical purposes of everyday life, our Ego in fact is nothing but a name for what is really only a sequence of observed behaviours.
author: Ananda Coomaraswamy

text: Each wave is born and is going to die, but the water is free from birth and death.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: The intuitive mind is a sacred gift and the rational mind is a faithful servant. We have created a society that honors the servant and has forgotten the gift.
author: Albert Einstein

text: When the opponent expands, I contract. When he contracts, I expand. And when there is an opportunity, I do not hit — it hits all by itself.
author: Bruce Lee

text: Here is my secret: I don't mind what happens.
author: J. Krishnamurti

text: If you can only be tall because someone else is on their knees, then you have a serious problem.
author: Toni Morrison

text: Be careful not to wear spiritualism as a badge to decorate your ego.
Thou canst not stir a flower without troubling of a star.
author: Francis Thompson

text: Trust in God, but tie your camel.
author: Prophet Muhammad

text: Awakened One, listen without distraction. Do not let your thoughts wander.
Meaning is a jumper that you have to knit yourself.
We spin cocoons around ourselves and get possessed by our possessions.
The forest was shrinking but the trees kept voting for the axe. For the axe was clever and convinced the trees that because his handle was wood he was one of them.
Hard times create strong men. Strong men create good times. Good times create weak men. And weak men create hard times.
author: G. Michael Hopf

text: Be like a tree. Let the dead leaves drop.
author: Rumi

text: Never make a permanent decision from a temporary emotion.
The places where you have the biggest challenges in your life become the places where you have the most to give.
author: Tracy McMillan

text: Wake up. To see the farm is to leave it.
All we ever want is the clues. Let people fill in the gaps.
What you seek is seeking you.
author: Rumi

text: Do not consider painful what is good for you.
author: Euripides

text: When the power of love overcomes the love of power, the world will know peace.
author: Jimi Hendrix

text: But war, organised war, is not a human instinct. It is a highly planned and cooperative form of theft.
Do not go where the path may lead. Go instead where there is no path and leave a trail.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: No snowflake ever falls in the wrong place.
author: Zen proverb

text: The only way out is through.
author: Robert Frost

text: You are not a drop in the ocean. You are the entire ocean in a drop.
author: Rumi

text: The privilege of a lifetime is to become who you truly are.
author: Carl Jung

text: The wound is the place where the light enters you.
author: Rumi

text: Between stimulus and response there is a space.
author: Viktor Frankl

text: Be patient toward all that is unsolved in your heart.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

text: The cave you fear to enter holds the treasure you seek.
author: Joseph Campbell

text: What we are looking for is what is looking.
author: Francis of Assisi

text: The only journey is the one within.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

text: Sit. Feast on your life.
author: Derek Walcott

text: What is essential is invisible to the eye.
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry

text: To study the Buddha Way is to study the self. To study the self is to forget the self. To forget the self is to be actualized by the myriad things.
author: Dogen

text: Each moment is all there is.
author: Dogen

text: When you find your place where you are, practice occurs.
author: Dogen

text: Enlightenment is intimacy with all things.
author: Dogen

text: In the beginner's mind there are many possibilities, in the expert's mind there are few.
author: Shunryu Suzuki

text: The most important thing is to find out what is the most important thing.
author: Shunryu Suzuki

text: Each of you is perfect the way you are, and you can use a little improvement.
author: Shunryu Suzuki

text: Zen is not some kind of excitement, but concentration on our usual everyday routine.
author: Shunryu Suzuki

text: Feelings come and go like clouds in a windy sky. Conscious breathing is my anchor.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: Walk as if you are kissing the Earth with your feet.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: The present moment is filled with joy and happiness. If you are attentive, you will see it.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: People have a hard time letting go of their suffering. Out of a fear of the unknown, they prefer suffering that is familiar.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: Sell your cleverness and buy bewilderment.
author: Rumi

text: Out beyond ideas of wrongdoing and rightdoing, there is a field. I'll meet you there.
author: Rumi

text: I have been a seeker and I still am, but I stopped asking the books and the stars. I started listening to the teaching of my soul.
author: Rumi

text: The quieter you become, the more you are able to hear.
author: Rumi

text: Let yourself be silently drawn by the strange pull of what you really love. It will not lead you astray.
author: Rumi

text: I wish I could show you, when you are lonely or in darkness, the astonishing light of your own being.
author: Daniel Ladinsky

text: Fear is the cheapest room in the house. I would like to see you living in better conditions.
author: Daniel Ladinsky

text: The place where you are right now, God circled on a map for you.
author: Daniel Ladinsky

text: Even after all this time, the sun never says to the earth, 'You owe me.' Look what happens with a love like that. It lights the whole sky.
author: Daniel Ladinsky

text: He who knows himself knows his Lord.
author: Ibn Arabi

text: My heart has become capable of every form: it is a pasture for gazelles and a convent for Christian monks, and a temple for idols and the pilgrim's Ka'ba, and the tables of the Torah and the book of the Quran.
author: Ibn Arabi

text: It is the truth that liberates, not your effort to be free.
author: Jiddu Krishnamurti

text: The ability to observe without evaluating is the highest form of intelligence.
author: Jiddu Krishnamurti

text: Freedom and love go together. Love is not a reaction. If I love you because you love me, that is mere trade.
author: Jiddu Krishnamurti

text: Wisdom is the ability, the readiness, to refuse what one wants to believe.
author: Nisargadatta Maharaj

text: The mind creates the abyss, the heart crosses it.
author: Nisargadatta Maharaj

text: Happiness is your nature. It is not wrong to desire it. What is wrong is seeking it outside when it is inside.
author: Ramana Maharshi

text: Silence is also conversation.
author: Ramana Maharshi

text: Your duty is to be; and not to be this or that.
author: Ramana Maharshi

text: Yoga is the cessation of the fluctuations of the mind.
author: Patanjali

text: You only lose what you cling to.
author: Gautama Buddha

text: Confine yourself to the present.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: You have power over your mind — not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The happiness of your life depends upon the quality of your thoughts.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Waste no more time arguing about what a good man should be. Be one.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
author: Seneca

text: It is not that we have a short time to live, but that we waste a lot of it.
author: Seneca

text: It is not what happens to you, but how you react to it that matters.
author: Epictetus

text: First say to yourself what you would be; and then do what you have to do.
author: Epictetus

text: No man is free who is not master of himself.
author: Epictetus

text: Everything flows.
author: Heraclitus

text: No man ever steps in the same river twice, for it's not the same river and he's not the same man.
author: Heraclitus

text: The soul is dyed the color of its thoughts.
author: Heraclitus

text: In the midst of winter, I found there was, within me, an invincible summer.
author: Albert Camus

text: The struggle itself toward the heights is enough to fill a man's heart. One must imagine Sisyphus happy.
author: Albert Camus

text: Real generosity toward the future lies in giving all to the present.
author: Albert Camus

text: Life can only be understood backwards; but it must be lived forwards.
author: Søren Kierkegaard

text: Anxiety is the dizziness of freedom.
author: Søren Kierkegaard

text: He who has a why to live for can bear almost any how.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: You must have chaos within you to give birth to a dancing star.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: The limits of my language mean the limits of my world.
author: Ludwig Wittgenstein

text: Whereof one cannot speak, thereof one must be silent.
author: Ludwig Wittgenstein

text: Attention is the rarest and purest form of generosity.
author: Simone Weil

text: All sins are attempts to fill voids.
author: Simone Weil

text: The only way to make sense out of change is to plunge into it, move with it, and join the dance.
author: Alan Watts

text: Until you make the unconscious conscious, it will direct your life and you will call it fate.
author: Carl Jung

text: Who looks outside, dreams; who looks inside, awakes.
author: Carl Jung

text: Everything that irritates us about others can lead us to an understanding of ourselves.
author: Carl Jung

text: There is no coming to consciousness without pain.
author: Carl Jung

text: Knowing your own darkness is the best method for dealing with the darknesses of other people.
author: Carl Jung

text: When an inner situation is not made conscious, it appears outside as fate.
author: Carl Jung

text: When we are no longer able to change a situation, we are challenged to change ourselves.
author: Viktor Frankl

text: Everything can be taken from a man but one thing: the last of the human freedoms — to choose one's attitude in any given set of circumstances.
author: Viktor Frankl

text: The curious paradox is that when I accept myself just as I am, then I can change.
author: Carl Rogers

text: What is most personal is most universal.
author: Carl Rogers

text: Tell me, what is it you plan to do with your one wild and precious life?
author: Mary Oliver

text: You do not have to be good. You do not have to walk on your knees for a hundred miles through the desert, repenting.
author: Mary Oliver

text: Pay attention. Be astonished. Tell about it.
author: Mary Oliver

text: Someone I loved once gave me a box full of darkness. It took me years to understand that this, too, was a gift.
author: Mary Oliver

text: The future enters into us, in order to transform itself in us, long before it happens.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

text: Live the questions now. Perhaps you will then gradually, without noticing it, live along some distant day into the answer.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

text: Perhaps all the dragons in our lives are princesses who are only waiting to see us act, just once, with beauty and courage.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

text: Tread softly because you tread on my dreams.
author: W. B. Yeats

text: I have spread my dreams under your feet.
author: W. B. Yeats

text: I celebrate myself, and sing myself.
author: Walt Whitman

text: Do I contradict myself? Very well then I contradict myself, (I am large, I contain multitudes.)
author: Walt Whitman

text: Re-examine all that you have been told. Dismiss that which insults your soul.
author: Walt Whitman

text: Caring for myself is not self-indulgence, it is self-preservation, and that is an act of political warfare.
author: Audre Lorde

text: Your silence will not protect you.
author: Audre Lorde

text: When I dare to be powerful, to use my strength in the service of my vision, then it becomes less and less important whether I am afraid.
author: Audre Lorde

text: If you are silent about your pain, they'll kill you and say you enjoyed it.
author: Zora Neale Hurston

text: Love takes off masks that we fear we cannot live without and know we cannot live within.
author: James Baldwin

text: Not everything that is faced can be changed, but nothing can be changed until it is faced.
author: James Baldwin

text: The most dangerous creation of any society is the man who has nothing to lose.
author: James Baldwin

text: If you are free, you need to free somebody else. If you have some power, then your job is to empower somebody else.
author: Toni Morrison

text: You wanna fly, you got to give up the shit that weighs you down.
author: Toni Morrison

text: There is a crack in everything. That's how the light gets in.
author: Leonard Cohen

text: Forget your perfect offering.
author: Leonard Cohen

text: And the day came when the risk to remain tight in a bud was more painful than the risk it took to blossom.
author: Anaïs Nin

text: We don't see things as they are, we see them as we are.
author: Anaïs Nin

text: Life shrinks or expands in proportion to one's courage.
author: Anaïs Nin

text: I was within and without, simultaneously enchanted and repelled by the inexhaustible variety of life.
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald

text: Reading is to the mind what exercise is to the body.
author: Joseph Addison

text: How wonderful it is that nobody need wait a single moment before starting to improve the world.
author: Anne Frank

text: We are what we pretend to be, so we must be careful about what we pretend to be.
author: Kurt Vonnegut

text: I urge you to please notice when you are happy, and exclaim or murmur or think at some point, 'If this isn't nice, I don't know what is.'
author: Kurt Vonnegut

text: I have always imagined that Paradise will be a kind of library.
author: Jorge Luis Borges

text: From the moment I picked your book up until I laid it down, I was convulsed with laughter. Some day I intend reading it.
author: Groucho Marx

text: A book must be the axe for the frozen sea within us.
author: Franz Kafka

text: You do not need to leave your room. Remain sitting at your table and listen.
author: Franz Kafka

text: How we spend our days is, of course, how we spend our lives.
author: Annie Dillard

text: We tell ourselves stories in order to live.
author: Joan Didion

text: I write entirely to find out what I'm thinking, what I'm looking at, what I see and what it means.
author: Joan Didion

text: God is not found in the soul by adding anything but by a process of subtraction.
author: Meister Eckhart

text: If the only prayer you ever say in your entire life is thank you, it will be enough.
author: Meister Eckhart

text: The eye through which I see God is the same eye through which God sees me.
author: Meister Eckhart

text: Let nothing disturb you. Let nothing frighten you. All things are passing. God never changes.
author: Teresa of Avila

text: Art is not what you see, but what you make others see.
author: Edgar Degas

text: Every child is an artist. The problem is how to remain an artist once we grow up.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: I shut my eyes in order to see.
author: Paul Gauguin

text: We do not grow absolutely, chronologically. We grow sometimes in one dimension, and not in another; unevenly.
author: Anaïs Nin

text: Start where you are. Use what you have. Do what you can.
author: Arthur Ashe

text: Hope is being able to see that there is light despite all of the darkness.
author: Desmond Tutu

text: Hope is the thing with feathers that perches in the soul.
author: Emily Dickinson

text: Tell all the truth but tell it slant.
author: Emily Dickinson

text: If I read a book and it makes my whole body so cold no fire can warm me, I know that is poetry.
author: Emily Dickinson

text: The truth will set you free, but first it will piss you off.
author: Gloria Steinem

text: Walking, ideally, is a state in which the mind, the body, and the world are aligned.
author: Rebecca Solnit

text: The love we want is the love we have to give.
author: Bell Hooks

text: Love is a combination of care, commitment, knowledge, responsibility, respect and trust.
author: Bell Hooks

text: Only when we are brave enough to explore the darkness will we discover the infinite power of our light.
author: Brené Brown

text: Vulnerability is the birthplace of innovation, creativity and change.
author: Brené Brown

text: Nothing ever goes away until it has taught us what we need to know.
author: Pema Chödrön

text: You are the sky. Everything else — it's just the weather.
author: Pema Chödrön

text: Compassion is not a relationship between the healer and the wounded. It's a relationship between equals.
author: Pema Chödrön

text: What you can plan is too small for you to live.
author: David Whyte

text: Anyone or anything that does not bring you alive is too small for you.
author: David Whyte

text: The antidote to exhaustion is not necessarily rest. The antidote to exhaustion is wholeheartedness.
author: David Whyte

text: It may be that when we no longer know what to do we have come to our real work, and that when we no longer know which way to go we have come to our real journey.
author: Wendell Berry

text: Be joyful though you have considered all the facts.
author: Wendell Berry

text: won't you celebrate with me what i have shaped into a kind of life? i had no model.
author: Lucille Clifton

text: we cannot live in the world if we are not willing to imagine the world.
author: Lucille Clifton

text: Before you know kindness as the deepest thing inside, you must know sorrow as the other deepest thing.
author: Naomi Shihab Nye

text: The river is famous to the fish.
author: Naomi Shihab Nye

text: The moment of change is the only poem.
author: Adrienne Rich

text: Lying is done with words, and also with silence.
author: Adrienne Rich

text: It astonishes me sometimes — no, often — how every person I get to know — everyone, regardless of everything, by which I mean everything — lives with some profound personal sorrow.
author: Ross Gay

text: The only thing to do is just notice. Eventually you will be a witness to all the wonders.
author: Marie Howe

text: I want to be easy in this world without forgetting it.
author: Ada Limón

text: What's the point of survival if you forbid yourself joy?
author: Ada Limón

text: Remember the sky that you were born under, know each of the star's stories.
author: Joy Harjo

text: To pray you open your whole self to sky, to earth, to sun, to moon, to one whole voice that is you.
author: Joy Harjo

text: Walking, I am listening to a deeper way. Suddenly all my ancestors are behind me. Be still, they say. Watch and listen. You are the result of the love of thousands.
author: Linda Hogan

text: Some people see scars, and it is wounding they remember. To me they are proof of the fact that there is healing.
author: Linda Hogan

text: Sometimes being offered tenderness feels like the very proof that you've been ruined.
author: Ocean Vuong

text: Sometimes it is necessary to reteach a thing its loveliness.
author: Galway Kinnell

text: I have walked through many lives, some of them my own, and I am not who I was, though some principle of being abides, from which I struggle not to stray.
author: Stanley Kunitz

text: Everything changes, and everything is connected. Pay attention. Make sure you are not careless with other people's hearts.
author: Jane Hirshfield

text: All the new thinking is about loss. In this it resembles all the old thinking.
author: Robert Hass

text: The world is charged with the grandeur of God.
author: Gerard Manley Hopkins

text: What I do is me: for that I came.
author: Gerard Manley Hopkins

text: Better by far you should forget and smile than that you should remember and be sad.
author: Christina Rossetti

text: Put your ear down close to your soul and listen hard.
author: Anne Sexton

text: Loneliness is the poverty of self; solitude is the richness of self.
author: May Sarton

text: If you don't know the kind of person I am and I don't know the kind of person you are, a pattern that others made may prevail in the world.
author: William Stafford

text: All flourishing is mutual.
author: Robin Wall Kimmerer

text: Knowing that you love the earth changes you, activates you to defend and protect and celebrate. But when you feel that the earth loves you in return, that feeling transforms the relationship from a one-way street into a sacred bond.
author: Robin Wall Kimmerer

text: Once in his life a man ought to concentrate his mind upon the remembered earth.
author: N. Scott Momaday

text: We do not exist for ourselves alone, and it is only when we are fully convinced of this fact that we begin to love ourselves properly.
author: Thomas Merton

text: You do not need to know precisely what is happening, or exactly where it is all going. What you need is to recognize the possibilities and challenges offered by the present moment, and to embrace them with courage, faith and hope.
author: Thomas Merton

text: Don't ask what the world needs. Ask what makes you come alive, and go do it. Because what the world needs is people who have come alive.
author: Howard Thurman

text: We do not think ourselves into new ways of living, we live ourselves into new ways of thinking.
author: Richard Rohr

text: The opposite of faith is not doubt; the opposite of faith is certainty.
author: Richard Rohr

text: The opposite of poverty is not wealth; the opposite of poverty is justice.
author: Bryan Stevenson

text: Each of us is more than the worst thing we've ever done.
author: Bryan Stevenson

text: We are the ones we have been waiting for.
author: June Jordan

text: All that you touch you change. All that you change changes you. The only lasting truth is change.
author: Octavia Butler

text: It is good to have an end to journey toward; but it is the journey that matters, in the end.
author: Ursula K. Le Guin

text: We live in capitalism. Its power seems inescapable. So did the divine right of kings.
author: Ursula K. Le Guin

text: Wherever you turn your eyes the world can shine like transfiguration.
author: Marilynne Robinson

text: You forget that the kindness of strangers is more often than not the only thing that gets you through the night.
author: Cormac McCarthy

text: Something will have gone out of us as a people if we ever let the remaining wilderness be destroyed.
author: Wallace Stegner

text: We are less damaged by the traumas of childhood than by the traumatic way we remember childhood.
author: James Hillman

text: The achievement of consciousness is the most heroic act of all.
author: Marion Woodman

text: Where you stumble, there lies your treasure.
author: Joseph Campbell

text: It is never too late to turn on the light.
author: Sharon Salzberg

text: Chaos should be regarded as extremely good news.
author: Chögyam Trungpa

text: We do not sit in meditation to become good meditators. We sit in meditation so that we'll be more awake in our lives.
author: Pema Chödrön

text: When we put down ideas of what life should be like, we are free to wholeheartedly say yes to our life as it is.
author: Tara Brach

text: Mindfulness is the awareness that arises from paying attention, on purpose, in the present moment, non-judgmentally.
author: Jon Kabat-Zinn

text: You can't stop the waves, but you can learn to surf.
author: Jon Kabat-Zinn

text: To be alive is to be vulnerable.
author: Madeleine L'Engle

text: It is not enough to be busy; so are the ants. The question is: what are we busy about?
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: I went to the woods because I wished to live deliberately, to front only the essential facts of life.
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: Beauty is not just a call to growth; it is a transforming presence wherein we unfold to ourselves.
author: John O'Donohue

text: Stress is a perverted relationship to time.
author: John O'Donohue

text: Hope locates itself in the premises that we don't know what will happen and that in the spaciousness of uncertainty is room to act.
author: Rebecca Solnit

text: Inside the word emergency is emerge; from an emergency new things come forth.
author: Rebecca Solnit

text: Critical thinking without hope is cynicism. Hope without critical thinking is naivete.
author: Maria Popova

text: Spend the afternoon. You can't take it with you.
author: Annie Dillard

text: There is no shortage of good days. It is good lives that are hard to come by.
author: Annie Dillard

text: Beauty and grace are performed whether or not we will or sense them. The least we can do is try to be there.
author: Annie Dillard

text: To be nobody but yourself in a world which is doing its best, night and day, to make you everybody else means to fight the hardest battle which any human being can fight; and never stop fighting.
author: E. E. Cummings

text: We are made from all those who have brought us food, and we cannot escape this in even our smallest acts.
author: N. Scott Momaday

text: Mystics do not deny the dark. They learn to navigate by it.
author: Mirabai Starr

text: Without forgiveness, there's no future.
author: Desmond Tutu
